text: This was like Sonic Mania back in 2008. A perfect retro revival - they went all out on making this feel authentic, down to the hilariously bad box art. IMO Megaman 9 is superior to any Megaman game on the NES for one reason - unlike those games, every weapon in this game is useful, with uses that aren't immediately obvious unless you get really good at the game. Also, the soundtrack is not just great chiptunes, but they sound like something Capcom would have composed at the time.

text:
6 year old comment and whatnot, but still
Mega Man 9 was an aptly, but very cheaply made, and quite blatant re telling of Mega Man 2. Was numbered as a sequel, yet purposefully skipped on the charge shot and slide to cash on nostalgia, and made headlines by having a woman in the roster. Which, surprise, hasn't happened again since.
The compositions don't sound like "something made at the time" - they are like Mega Man 2's. Except not as good.

Capcom made every last effort to cash on the retro boom, and for it to hit it's maximum, essentially redid 2, ignoring the entire rest of the series.

The result was a game with some really bullshit sections that didn't have an identity of its own. 10 didn't fare much better but at least it was more creative.
